business.headline: Kotak Mahindra Bank MD and CEO Ashok Vaswani to step down at year-endKotak Mahindra Bank has begun searching for a successor after CEO Ashok Vaswani said he will not seek reappointment when his term ends in December 2026. The move comes as the lender seeks faster growth after regulatory scrutiny and may keep investors focused on leadership continuity.

NSE, Jio IPOs have retail investors excited. But are big listings worth the hype?NSE and Jio are already generating plenty of excitement. But before investors join the rush, recent IPO history offers an important lesson about hype, valuations and long-term returns. Read on to find out why big listings don't always translate into big gains.

Will life insurance pay after a fire? Here's what every family should knowRecent fire tragedies have triggered questions over whether life insurance pays after accidental deaths. Experts say standard term plans generally cover such deaths unless false disclosures or policy exclusions apply.

Asian markets crash, will Dalal Street feel the aftershock on Monday?From South Korea's Kospi to Japan's Nikkei, Asian stock markets have tumbled sharply, leaving investors on edge. As Dalal Street prepares to reopen after a three-day holiday on Monday, will the BSE and NSE also open deep in the red?

From ITR filing to TDS: 3 major tax deadlines in July 2026 you shouldn't missWhether you're a salaried employee, a business owner or an employer, keeping track of these dates can help you avoid last-minute stress. Here are three key tax deadlines you should not miss in July 2026.

Will your family get your EPF pension after your death? Here's what you need to knowNo one likes to think about what happens after they're gone. But if you're an EPF member, knowing whether your family can continue receiving your pension could make a big difference to their financial future.

Will DA rise before the 8th Pay Commission? Here's what the latest data suggestsThe wait for the 8th Pay Commission continues, but all eyes are now on the next DA revision. As inflation remains elevated and the July review draws closer, is another DA hike just around the corner?

Govt restores commercial LPG supply to pre-West Asia crisis levelsThe move comes after the government reviewed the country's fuel availability and found that both domestic production and imported LPG supplies have improved.

AI reality check hits Asia as Seoul's Kospi crashes 8%, Tokyo's Nikkei tumbles 5%South Korea's KOSPI fell 8.2%, prompting authorities to halt program trading for 20 minutes after steep losses rippled across the market. Japan's Nikkei 225 dropped 5%, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng declined 2.4%.

What's the one big hurdle delaying India-US trade deal? Piyush Goyal explainsPiyush Goyal said India will bring the US trade deal into force only after Washington creates a tariff framework that favours Indian exporters over regional competitors. The position follows US legal changes that overturned reciprocal tariffs and forced both sides to revisit implementation.

Sebi may allow celebrity endorsements for financial brands. But here's a catchUnder the proposal, celebrities would be allowed to endorse a financial firm's brand or company name, subject to prescribed conditions, disclaimers and prior approvals.

Stock market holiday on Muharram: Are NSE and BSE open for trading on June 26?Planning to trade on Friday, June 26? With Muharram being observed across the country, many investors are wondering whether the NSE and BSE will remain open for trading?

Don't want to pay taxes online? Here's how to use the 'Pay at Bank Counter' optionNot comfortable paying taxes online? You're not alone. The Income Tax Department still allows taxpayers to pay their dues at authorised bank branches through the 'Pay at Bank Counter' facility.

India-US trade deal very close, but here's why it isn't signed yetPiyush Goyal said India and the US are close to finalising their trade deal. India wants tariff terms that give its exporters a clear edge over rivals.

Dalal Street pares gains: Sensex ends flat after climbing over 600 pointsEarly optimism driven by lower crude oil prices and a stronger rupee faded as investors booked profits, though benchmark indices still managed to end in positive territory.

Zerodha's Nithin Kamath has a warning for you against pyramid schemesAccording to figures cited by Kamath, more than 5.5 crore Indians have lost their savings to over 5,300 such schemes. The estimated losses stood at around Rs 10 lakh crore as of 2015, and he suggested that the actual figure could be much higher now.

Gold loses over Rs 51,000 from all-time high. Why is the yellow metal crashing?Gold has fallen sharply from its peak as silver also comes under selling pressure. The correction is being driven by rate-hike bets, a stronger dollar and shifting investor appetite.

United Spirits may cut 100 jobs as Diageo looks to trim costsUnited Spirits is set to lay off around 100 employees as Diageo pursues a global cost-cutting programme. The move comes amid a wider restructuring drive in India despite strong growth and continued investment in premium brands.

Amazon to invest $48 billion in India by 2030, CEO Andy Jassy meets PM ModiAccording to Jassy, the company aims to support 3.8 million jobs in India by 2030 and help generate $80 billion in cumulative e-commerce exports.

Sensex jumps over 600 points: Why are stock markets rallying today?Benchmark indices extended their recovery on Thursday as softer crude, firm Asian cues and gains in banks and autos lifted sentiment. The move has eased near-term macro concerns, though investors remain watchful of the monsoon and global central bank signals.

entertainment.headline: Never miss my target: Ssunita hints at 'third party' in Govinda shooting incident Ssunita Ahuja made a cryptic remark about Govinda's accidental shooting incident, hinting at a "third party" in his life. She also claimed that the actor had "many affairs" during their marriage while reflecting on their relationship.

Did Kriti Sanon and Kabir Bahia call it quits?Here's the truth behind viral photo A viral photograph of Kabir Bahia with another woman sparked fresh speculation about his rumoured relationship with Kriti Sanon. But is there any truth to the breakup rumours? Here's what a source has revealed.

Sharvari, Vedang Raina watch Main Vaapas Aaunga in theatre, thank fans in a note Actor Sharvari shared a heartfelt note after watching Main Vaapas Aaunga with co-star Vedang Raina in a theatre. The actor said the love for Jiya and Keenu has helped the Imtiaz Ali directorial reach more hearts with each passing week.

Friends star Courteney Cox, Johnny McDaid split after 11 years? Here's what we know Friends star Courteney Cox and Snow Patrol musician Johnny McDaid have reportedly ended their 11-year relationship. According to a report, the split was amicable, with the couple remaining close friends despite deciding to go their separate ways.

Actor Harshad Chopda recalls being cheated on by his girlfriend with his best friend Television actor Harshad Chopda made an emotional confession on Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa, revealing that his girlfriend cheated on him with his best friend. The actor said the betrayal, which happened in 2010, left him unable to trust people and that he had kept the pain to himself for 16 years.

Breaking down box office maths: Popcorn or tickets, what pays theatre bills? Ever wondered where your movie ticket money actually goes? In today's piece, we decode the business behind the big screen – from ticket splits and popcorn profits to why multiplexes and single-screen theatres play by different rules.

Laapataa Ladies actor Satendra Soni accuses filmmaker of assault over payment dispute Laapataa Ladies actor Satendra Soni has alleged that he was assaulted and threatened by a filmmaker over unpaid dues during a shoot in Madhya Pradesh. In an emotional Instagram video, the actor claimed he was denied payment after eight days of filming.

Cocktail 2 box office Day 9: Shahid's film surpasses lifetime haul of 2012 original Cocktail 2 has surpassed the lifetime India nett collection of the 2012 hit Cocktail, earning Rs 78.75 crore domestically in nine days. Despite the milestone, the Shahid Kapoor, Rashmika Mandanna and Kriti Sanon-starrer has witnessed a steady decline in collections after its opening weekend.

Welcome To The Jungle box office Day 2: Akshay's film crosses Rs 50 crore globally Akshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle has crossed the Rs 50 crore mark at the worldwide box office in just two days. The film collected Rs 20 crore nett on Saturday, taking its India nett total to Rs 39 crore and worldwide gross to Rs 57.50 crore.

Harry Styles collapses on stage after choking while drinking water during concert Harry Styles suffered a brief choking scare after accidentally choking while drinking water during his Wembley Stadium concert in London. He collapsed on stage for a few seconds before recovering.

Actor Akanksha Chamola confirms divorce from Gaurav Khanna on Lock Upp Season 2 Akanksha Chamola said on Lock Upp Season 2 that she and Gaurav Khanna have been living separately for a year and are headed for divorce. The actor's latest revelation has also renewed attention on the couple's earlier conversations about parenthood.

Actor Sonu Mishra quits Kala Hiran amid Salman Khan's legal battle Actor Sonu Mishra has stepped away from Kala Hiran: The Battle for Legacy, saying the film was designed to tarnish a senior actor's image. His exit comes as Salman Khan's legal challenge against the project continues.

Khushbu Sundar defends wedding post amid backlash after K Bhagyaraj's death Khushbu Sundar hit back at criticism over her wedding-day post after K Bhagyaraj's death. The reaction came as condolences and a final video from her daughter's Goa wedding spread online.

Suniel Shetty shares granddaughter Evaarah's daily ritual involving PM Modi's photo Suniel Shetty has shared an unusual daily ritual involving his 15-month-old granddaughter Evaarah and a photograph of Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Here's what the actor revealed and why he believes it is significant.

Five-time award-winning cinematographer Dillip Ray dies at 72 after brief illness Cinematographer Dillip Ray has died at 72. Read about his remarkable journey, celebrated films, award-winning career and the tributes pouring in after his death.

Ujjwal Nikam praises Rajkummar Rao for bringing his story to life in Prahaar Public Prosecutor Ujjwal Nikam has praised Rajkummar Rao and Avinash Arun for bringing his story to screen in Prahaar. He said the film captures landmark prosecutions as well as the private loneliness behind them.

Neeraj Ghaywan criticises TVF's Panchayat for featuring 'only upper-caste names' Filmmaker Neeraj Ghaywan has questioned the authenticity of TVF's Panchayat over its portrayal of village life. Here's what he said and why his remarks have sparked discussion.

Jackky Bhagnani's old dating app profile goes viral after situationship remarks Jackky Bhagnani is back in the spotlight after old screenshots of his alleged Raya profile resurfaced on Reddit, months after his "situationship" remark about wife Rakul Preet Singh sparked controversy.

Suniel Shetty on Don 3 row: Ranveer Singh brought Rs 3,500 crore to the industry Actor Suniel Shetty has come out in support of Ranveer Singh amid the Don 3 controversy, questioning calls to ban the actor and making a strong case for artistic freedom. Here's what he said.

general news.headline: Look inwards, act against terror networks: India rejects Pak's Karachi blast claimIn a sharp response, the Ministry of External Affairs said Pakistan should stop "pointing fingers at others" and instead take credible action against terrorist networks functioning within its territory.

PM Modi awarded Seychelles' highest honour for environmental leadershipPrime Minister Narendra Modi has received the 'Guardian of the Blue Horizon', Seychelles' highest environmental honour, for leadership in conservation and sustainable development. Calling it a tribute to India's people, he reaffirmed the country's commitment to climate action, green growth and global environmental cooperation.

What happened at Pune fort? Cops recreate Ketan's murder scene with SiyaSiya Goyal was taken to the Lohagad Fort at 6.30 am on Sunday. According to the police, the exercise was aimed at recreating the exact chain of events around the deadly fall that claimed Ketan Agarwal's life on June 18.

Man sets off bomb in car to die together with ex; she jumps out, he meets fiery endA woman escaped from a moving cab in Karnataka's Tumakuru moments before an explosion killed her ex-boyfriend inside. Police said a bomb-like material was found in the vehicle.

Why men looking for sex reach Congress House in MumbaiMumbai's Congress House, the pre-Independence-era landmark, is not a brothel, but men driven by lust and desire often fail to make the distinction. The former Congress office's proximity to a mujra house and the red-light district of Kamathipura make it carry a stigma. However, it is not really untouched by the sex trade.

Two photos capture horror of Palestinian journalist's months in Israeli captivityThe striking transformation of Palestinian journalist Mujahid Bani Mufleh before and after detention has reignited debate over Israeli prison conditions. A prisoner rights group claims he suffered abuse and medical neglect, allegations Israel has previously rejected.

World looking at another inflation, this time blame RAM and not oilIf you buy a laptop or phone this year, you would pay 20 to 30 per cent more compared to last year. There is a RAM-driven inflation in the tech world, it is going to make everything more expensive.

This is donkey trading: BJP slams MDMK after it dumps DMK for Vijay's TVKIn a major shift in Tamil Nadu's political landscape, MDMK chief Vaiko on Saturday announced that his party would snap ties with the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance and align with Chief Minister Vijay's TVK.

Honour above victory: Iran bid goodbye to FIFA World Cup with emotional noteIran's hopes of reaching the FIFA World Cup knockout stage for the first time ended in heartbreak after Austria's last-gasp equaliser against Algeria sealed their exit. But instead of focusing on elimination, Iran signed off with an emotional message on honour, fairness and standing tall despite missing out.

2 Indian army men script history, win first-ever gold in World Rowing CupIndia created history at the 2026 World Rowing Cup III in Lucerne after Lakshay and Ujjwal Kumar Singh won the country's first-ever gold medal at the event. The Indian Army rowers topped the Lightweight Men's Double Sculls final to secure a landmark result for Indian rowing.

Music, dancing, then screams: Man dives headfirst into resort swimming pool, diesA holiday trip to a resort ended in tragedy for a 25-year-old tourist, who died while diving into a shallow swimming pool. The June 20 incident has drawn widespread attention after a video of the fatal dive surfaced online days later.

Bengaluru Court summons Priyank Kharge, Mohammed Nalpad in RSS defamation caseA Bengaluru court has taken cognisance of a private defamation complaint against Priyank Kharge and Mohammed Nalpad over alleged remarks on the RSS. The order starts criminal proceedings against the two leaders while dropping the complaint against Dinesh Gundu Rao.

India makes hydrogen from nuclear heat, not electricity. Why scientists are thrilledIndia has inaugurated the world's first plant that makes hydrogen using nuclear reactor heat instead of electricity. Built at Kalpakkam, the copper-chlorine cycle points to a cleaner, round-the-clock route to green hydrogen.

Sport has never had more greatness. Have we forgotten how to watch?Handcrafted ByOur Editors From Messi and Ronaldo to Federer, Nadal and Djokovic, modern sport has handed us an embarrassment of riches. Instead of enjoying it, we keep reaching for the scoreboard. Why has the GOAT debate become a habit we can't seem to break?

Will face hell: Iran's stark warning to US, Gulf nations amid escalating attacksIran launched missile and drone strikes on US military sites in Kuwait and Bahrain after fresh American attacks on Iranian targets. As tensions surged despite a fragile ceasefire, US President Donald Trump warned Iran could "cease to exist" if Washington is forced to escalate its military response.

Mumbai cops uncover Muharram poisoning plot, seize 14,900 rat poison capsulesMumbai Police detained a Pune businessman after seizing 14,900 capsules allegedly filled with zinc phosphide during a Muharram procession. Investigators are now examining his travels, finances and contacts for possible wider links.

Breaking down box office maths: Popcorn or tickets, what pays theatre bills?Ever wondered where your movie ticket money actually goes? In today's piece, we decode the business behind the big screen – from ticket splits and popcorn profits to why multiplexes and single-screen theatres play by different rules.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Heat index, not the thermometer, is why Delhi feels like 51°C. What is it?Delhi's "feels like" temperature hit 51.3 degrees Celsius on Sunday, June 28, even though the thermometer read just 41.3 degrees Celsius. Here is what the heat index means, the simple maths behind the formula, and why humidity makes the capital feel so much hotter.

Old video shows Siya leaning on Chetan as they watch cricket match togetherThe probe into Pune realtor Ketan Agarwal's murder has uncovered another link between accused Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary, with police finding that the duo were seen together at an MFCL cricket match before the crime.

Watch: Lightning bolt strikes Eiffel Tower. Here's how Paris keeps it safeA lightning bolt struck the Eiffel Tower during a thunderstorm over Paris, lighting up the skyline. The spectacle drew attention online, even as experts noted the landmark is designed to withstand such strikes.

India now dominates global research paper retractions with 6 universities in top 10India's rise in global research rankings is now shadowed by a sharp spike in paper retractions. With six Indian universities among the world's top 10 for retractions, concerns around plagiarism, fake peer reviews, and flawed incentives are growing, raising questions about credibility, funding, and the future of Indian science.

Live: PM Modi calls his address at Seychelles Parliament a 'special honour'PM Modi in Seychelles Live Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Sunday received a ceremonial Guard of Honour at the State House in Victoria before holding bilateral talks with Seychelles President Dr Patrick Herminie. During the visit, Modi was also conferred the 'Guardian of the Blue Horizon', Seychelles' highest honour for leadership in environmental conservation and sustainable development. He is attending the island nation's 50th National Day celebrations as the Guest of Honour, with an Indian Army marching contingent from the Assam Regiment, alongside an Indian Navy contingent and military band, taking part in the Golden Jubilee parade. On Saturday, Modi described the India-Seychelles partnership as "steady, strong and long-lasting" at a state dinner hosted by President Herminie, who hailed India as a "steadfast friend" and key development partner. Modi also handed over the India-built fast patrol vessel Lespwar and other assistance to bolster Seychelles' security and development. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for all the latest updates.

Jana Nayagan producer appointed Tamil Nadu Special Representative to DelhiTamil Nadu has appointed K Venkata Narayana as its Special Representative in New Delhi for one year. The move has drawn opposition criticism over his film industry background and proximity to C Joseph Vijay.

sports.headline: Sport has never had more greatness. Have we forgotten how to watch? From Messi and Ronaldo to Federer, Nadal and Djokovic, modern sport has handed us an embarrassment of riches. Instead of enjoying it, we keep reaching for the scoreboard. Why has the GOAT debate become a habit we can't seem to break?

2 Indian army men script history, win first-ever gold in World Rowing Cup India created history at the 2026 World Rowing Cup III in Lucerne after Lakshay and Ujjwal Kumar Singh won the country's first-ever gold medal at the event. The Indian Army rowers topped the Lightweight Men's Double Sculls final to secure a landmark result for Indian rowing.

Haaland drops cheeky message as Argentina maintain unbeaten run in World Cup Erling Haaland has dropped a cheeky message after Lionel Messi's record-extending 19th goal in the FIFA World Cup. Haaland's hilarious message has gone viral on the internet.

Honour above victory: Iran bid goodbye to FIFA World Cup with emotional note Iran's hopes of reaching the FIFA World Cup knockout stage for the first time ended in heartbreak after Austria's last-gasp equaliser against Algeria sealed their exit. But instead of focusing on elimination, Iran signed off with an emotional message on honour, fairness and standing tall despite missing out.

Novak Djokovic eyes Wimbledon history with Serena Williams as inspiration Novak Djokovic hailed Serena Williams' Wimbledon comeback as "inspirational and epic", praising the American great's determination to return at 44 as he chases history at the All England Club.

Argentina march on as Messi makes history, Algeria, Austria progress after epic draw History, heartbreak and late drama defined the final day in Group J as Lionel Messi broke another World Cup record, Argentina completed a perfect campaign and Austria's last-gasp equaliser sent both themselves and Algeria into the Round of 32 at Iran's expense.

World Cup: Full Round of 32 matches decided after epic finish to group stage The FIFA World Cup Round of 32 matches have been finalised after a thrilling final day of the group stage. The final two places in the knockouts were decided after Algeria and Austria played out an epic 3-3 thriller.

Are India equipped enough to script another World Cup classic against Australia? Women's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Harmanpreet Kaur's India will take on Sophie Molineux's Australia in a blockbuster Group A clash at the iconic Lord's Cricket Ground on Sunday.

2nd T20I: Will India unleash Sooryavanshi in search for a series-saving win? Ireland vs India, 2nd T20I preview: Stunned by a historic Irish victory in the Belfast opener, a fumbling Indian side faces a defining selection dilemma as Shreyas Iyer seeks to protect a proud three-year unbeaten T20I series streak.

Cristiano Ronaldo's quiet night exposes Portugal's biggest World Cup problem FIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal were held 0-0 by Colombia as Cristiano Ronaldo was left chasing service in Miami. The draw sent Portugal through as runners-up but sharpened concerns over their midfield and identity before the Croatia clash.

DR Congo end 52-year wait with historic win, set up England clash in last 32FIFA World Cup: DR Congo booked a historic place in the knockout stage and set up a round-of-32 clash with England after producing a stirring comeback from a goal down to beat Uzbekistan 3-1 in Atlanta on Saturday.

Harry Kane rewrites England World Cup history, overtakes Ronaldo in goals tallyHarry Kane etched his name deeper into England's football history after becoming the country's highest goalscorer in FIFA World Cup history during the Three Lions' 2-0 win over Panama on Sunday.

Highlights: Argentina, Austria and Algeria reach knockoutsJordan (JOR) vs Argentina (ARG) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi scored his 19th World Cup goal to help Argentina finish unbeaten in the group stage with a 3-1 win over Jordan while Austria equalise with the last kick of the game, sending both teams through to the round of 32 after one of the most insane games of World Cup football you will ever see. Iran is out.

Thomas Tuchel gets the job done as Jude Bellingham downs stubborn PanamaPanama vs England, FIFA World Cup 2026: Jude Bellingham starred with a goal and an assist as England beat Panama 2-0 to top Group L at the FIFA World Cup. Harry Kane became England's all-time leading World Cup scorer as Croatia also advanced.

COL vs POR Highlights: Portugal survive Colombia after 0-0 drawColombia (COL) vs Portugal (POR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Diogo Costa's string of stunning saves helped Portugal survive a relentless Colombian assault, but it was Colombia who topped Group K after a goalless draw in Miami. DR Congo's win over Uzbekistan ensured all three sides progressed to the Round of 32 from a dramatic finale.

Cody Gakpo stays with Netherlands squad after heartbreaking loss of unborn sonFIFA World Cup 2026: Cody Gakpo's World Cup journey has been overshadowed by unimaginable personal tragedy after the Netherlands forward and partner Noa van der Bij announced the loss of their unborn son. Despite the heartbreak, Gakpo has chosen to remain with the Dutch squad for the knockout stages.

DR Congo's Lumumba fan denied US visa for must-win clash vs UzbekistanDR Congo's biggest match at the FIFA World Cup 2026 will be missing one of its most recognisable supporters. Cult fan Michel Kuka Mboladinga, famous for his Patrice Lumumba tribute, has been denied a US visa and will miss Saturday's crucial clash against Uzbekistan.

PAN 0-2 ENG: England and Croatia make it through to the knockoutsPanama vs England Live score, FIFA World Cup 2026: England sealed top spot in Group L with a 2-0 win over Panama as Jude Bellingham and Harry Kane struck, while Croatia beat Ghana 2-1 to join the Three Lions in the World Cup Round of 32.

Rachin Ravindra stars as Day 3 swings New Zealand's way in series deciderRachin Ravindra's unbeaten 60 guided New Zealand to a 204-run lead over England on Day 3 of the series decider at Trent Bridge, putting the Black Caps firmly in control heading into Day 4.

Sophie Molineux enjoying added captaincy role in Australia's dominant campaignWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Speaking to the reporters before the match against India, Sophie Molineux said that the ongoing tournament has been different, but she has enjoyed the added responsibility of captaincy after replacing Alyssa Healy.

Smriti Mandhana urges India to bat with more intent in must-win Australia clashWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Speaking to the reports, Smriti Mandhana said that India need to show more intent with the way if they are to beat Sophie Molineux's Australia on Sunday and qualify for the semis.

Ireland claim first-ever win in Women's T20 World Cup day after men's India upsetIreland Women created history by registering their first-ever Women's T20 World Cup victory, defeating West Indies by six wickets to end a 21-match losing streak, just a day after the Ireland men's team stunned India in Bristol.

Ex-Pakistan captain Sana Mir slams sexist remarks at women's teamFormer Pakistan captain Sana Mir has slammed the sexist backlash against the women's cricket team after its Women's T20 World Cup exit, urging fans to replace gender-based attacks with constructive criticism.

Ancy Sojan breaks Anju Bobby George's long jump national recordAncy Sojan shattered Anju Bobby George's 22-year-old women's long jump national record with a historic 6.88m leap at the National Inter State Senior Athletics Championships on Saturday.

Sarvesh Kushare smashes high jump national record, books Asian Games berthSarvesh Kushare set a new men's high jump national record by clearing 2.31m to win gold at the National Inter-State Athletics Championships in Bhubaneswar, eclipsing Tejaswin Shankar's eight-year-old mark of 2.29m.

India under pressure, Australia ready to make their life tougher: Sophie MolineuxWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Australia captain Sophie Molineux said her side will look to capitalise on the pressure India are under in Sunday's crucial Group A clash at Lord's Cricket Ground.

No money for equipment, Sindhushree breaks national record with borrowed poleG Sindhushree broke the national pole vault record with a borrowed pole to win gold and seal an Asian Games berth. She dedicated the achievement to her late father, who had backed her sporting journey.

India will draw confidence vs Australia from recent T20I series win: Shafali VermaWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Shafali Verma believes India will draw confidence from their recent 2-1 T20I series victory over Australia on Australian soil going into the Lord's clash.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
